---
title: "fig3"
format: html
editor: visual
---

### Panel A

```{r}
library(ape)
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(ggtree))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(treeio))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(dplyr))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(ggplot2))

# Load trees
tree1 <- read.tree("NTERM_IQTREE/NTERM_no_partition.treefile")
tree2 <- read.tree("Species_tree/Species_Tree_With_tags.treefile")

# Clean tip labels:
tree1$tip.label <- gsub("^([^|]+)\\|.*", "\\1", tree1$tip.label)
tree2$tip.label <-  gsub("^(Spec\\d+\\|[^/]+).*", "\\1", tree2$tip.label)

# Unroot and prune to shared tips
shared <- intersect(tree1$tip.label, tree2$tip.label)
tree1 <- unroot(drop.tip(tree1, setdiff(tree1$tip.label, shared)))
tree2 <- unroot(drop.tip(tree2, setdiff(tree2$tip.label, shared)))

# Sort tips for alignment
tree1 <- ladderize(tree1)
tree2 <- ladderize(tree2)

# Left tree
p1 <- ggtree(tree1) + geom_tiplab(size = 2, hjust = 1)
p1_data <- p1$data %>% filter(isTip)

# Right tree (flipped horizontally)
p2 <- ggtree(tree2) + geom_tiplab(size = 2, hjust = 0)
p2_data <- p2$data %>% filter(isTip)

# Align by tip labels
tip_links <- inner_join(p1_data, p2_data, by = "label", suffix = c(".left", ".right"))

# Plot the tanglegram
ggplot() +
  # Left tree
  geom_tree(data = p1$data, aes(x = -x, y = y), size = 0.4) +

  # Right tree
  geom_tree(data = p2$data, aes(x = x + max(p1$data$x) + 1, y = y), size = 0.4) +

  # Connecting lines
  geom_curve(data = tip_links,
             aes(x = -x.left, y = y.left,
                 xend = x.right + max(p1$data$x) + 1, yend = y.right),
             curvature = 0.2,
             alpha = 0.05,
             color = "steelblue",
             size = 0.3) +

  # Tree labels
  annotate("text",
           x = -max(p1$data$x) - 1,
           y = max(p1$data$y) + 5,
           label = "NTERM",
           hjust = 0,
           size = 5,
           fontface = "bold") +

  annotate("text",
           x = max(p2$data$x) + max(p1$data$x) + 2,
           y = max(p2$data$y) + 5,
           label = "Species tree",
           hjust = 1,
           size = 5,
           fontface = "bold") +

  theme_void()


#ggsave("NTERM_IQTREE/ggtree_tanglegram_NTERM_vs_SpeciesTree.pdf", width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300)

```

### **Panel B (CenH3):**

Box plots: dN/dS for HFD vs N-terminal tail - Show N-tail has higher evolutionary rates -

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)

# Read the data, skipping lines starting with #
data <- read.table("sites_rates/Histone_Curated_Alignment_Unique_Ids_cenH3_nopartition.rate", 
                   header = TRUE, 
                   comment.char = "#")

# Create region categories
data$Region <- ifelse(data$Site <= 46, "N-term (1-46)", 
                      ifelse(data$Site <= 149, "HFD (47-149)", "Other"))

# Filter to only include N-term and HFD regions
data_filtered <- data[data$Region %in% c("N-term (1-46)", "HFD (47-149)"), ]

# Set factor levels to control order (N-term first, then HFD)
data_filtered$Region <- factor(data_filtered$Region, 
                               levels = c("N-term (1-46)", "HFD (47-149)"))

# Perform Wilcoxon rank-sum test
nterm_rates <- data_filtered$Rate[data_filtered$Region == "N-term (1-46)"]
hfd_rates <- data_filtered$Rate[data_filtered$Region == "HFD (47-149)"]
wilcox_test <- wilcox.test(nterm_rates, hfd_rates)

# Print test results
cat("Wilcoxon Rank-Sum Test Results:\n")
cat("W statistic:", wilcox_test$statistic, "\n")
cat("P-value:", wilcox_test$p.value, "\n")
cat("N-term median:", median(nterm_rates), "\n")
cat("HFD median:", median(hfd_rates), "\n\n")

# Create boxplot
p <- ggplot(data_filtered, aes(x = Region, y = Rate, fill = Region)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = 21, outlier.size = 2.5, outlier.stroke = 0.8,
               alpha = 0.8, linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "Substitution Rates: N-terminal vs HFD",
       subtitle = sprintf("Wilcoxon test: p = %.2e", wilcox_test$p.value),
       x = "",
       y = "Substitution Rate") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, color = "gray30"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
        axis.line = element_line(linewidth = 0.8),
        axis.ticks = element_line(linewidth = 0.8)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("N-term (1-46)" = "grey", 
                                "HFD (47-149)" = "white"))

# Display plot
print(p)

# Save plot
#ggsave("sites_rates/Histone_Curated_Alignment_Unique_Ids_cenH3_nopartition.rate_boxplot.png", p, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#ggsave("sites_rates/Histone_Curated_Alignment_Unique_Ids_cenH3_nopartition.rate_boxplot.pdf", 
#       p, 
#       width = 8, 
#       height = 6, 
#       device = cairo_pdf)



```

### **Panel B (Canonical H3):**

(CanH3) Evolutionary rate comparison - Box plots: dN/dS for HFD vs N-terminal tail

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)

# Read the data, skipping lines starting with #
data <- read.table("sites_rates/Histone_Curated_Alignment_Unique_Ids_canH3_nopartition.rate", 
                   header = TRUE, 
                   comment.char = "#")

# Create region categories
data$Region <- ifelse(data$Site <= 46, "N-term (1-46)", 
                      ifelse(data$Site <= 149, "HFD (47-149)", "Other"))

# Filter to only include N-term and HFD regions
data_filtered <- data[data$Region %in% c("N-term (1-46)", "HFD (47-149)"), ]

# Set factor levels to control order (N-term first, then HFD)
data_filtered$Region <- factor(data_filtered$Region, 
                               levels = c("N-term (1-46)", "HFD (47-149)"))

# Perform Wilcoxon rank-sum test
nterm_rates <- data_filtered$Rate[data_filtered$Region == "N-term (1-46)"]
hfd_rates <- data_filtered$Rate[data_filtered$Region == "HFD (47-149)"]
wilcox_test <- wilcox.test(nterm_rates, hfd_rates)

# Print test results
cat("Wilcoxon Rank-Sum Test Results:\n")
cat("W statistic:", wilcox_test$statistic, "\n")
cat("P-value:", wilcox_test$p.value, "\n")
cat("N-term median:", median(nterm_rates), "\n")
cat("HFD median:", median(hfd_rates), "\n\n")

# Create boxplot

p <- ggplot(data_filtered, aes(x = Region, y = Rate, fill = Region)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = 21, outlier.size = 2.5, outlier.stroke = 0.8,
               alpha = 0.8, linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "Substitution Rates: N-terminal vs HFD",
       subtitle = sprintf("Wilcoxon test: p = %.2e", wilcox_test$p.value),
       x = "",
       y = "Substitution Rate") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, color = "gray30"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
        axis.line = element_line(linewidth = 0.8),
        axis.ticks = element_line(linewidth = 0.8)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("N-term (1-46)" = "grey", 
                                "HFD (47-149)" = "white")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 4), breaks = seq(0, 4, 1))


# Display plot
print(p)

# Save plot
#ggsave("sites_rates/Histone_Curated_Alignment_Unique_Ids_canH3_nopartition.rate_boxplot.png", p, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# Save plot

#ggsave("sites_rates/Histone_Curated_Alignment_Unique_Ids_canH3_nopartition.rate_boxplot.pdf", 
#       p, 
#       width = 8, 
#       height = 6, 
#       device = cairo_pdf)
```

## Panel C-D

Panel C: Length variation • Histogram of N-terminal tail lengths across fungi • Color by clade • Show extreme length variation (e.g., 50-300 amino acids)

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(seqinr)
library(dplyr)

# File paths
taxonomy_file <- "histone_class_taxonomy_mapping_revised.csv"
#fasta_file <- "Histone_Curated_Alignment_Unique_Ids_cenH3.fasta"
fasta_file <- "NTERM_IQTREE/Histone_Curated_Alignment_Unique_Ids_NTERM_revised_CenH3.fasta"
# Read taxonomy mapping
taxonomy <- read.csv(taxonomy_file, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

cat("Taxonomy entries:", nrow(taxonomy), "\n\n")

# Read FASTA file
fasta_data <- read.fasta(fasta_file, seqtype = "AA", as.string = TRUE)

cat("FASTA sequences:", length(fasta_data), "\n\n")

# Function to calculate N-terminal length (position before first non-gap character)
calculate_nterm_length <- function(seq_string) {
  # Remove gaps
  seq_nogaps <- gsub("-", "", seq_string)
  # Return length of ungapped sequence (total protein length)
  return(nchar(seq_nogaps))
}

# Function to calculate amino acid composition
calculate_aa_composition <- function(seq_string) {
  # Remove gaps
  seq_nogaps <- gsub("-", "", seq_string)
  
  # Count each amino acid
  aa_list <- c("A", "C", "D", "E", "F", "G", "H", "I", "K", "L", 
               "M", "N", "P", "Q", "R", "S", "T", "V", "W", "Y")
  
  total_aa <- nchar(seq_nogaps)
  if (total_aa == 0) return(rep(0, length(aa_list)))
  
  composition <- sapply(aa_list, function(aa) {
    (nchar(seq_nogaps) - nchar(gsub(aa, "", seq_nogaps))) / total_aa * 100
  })
  
  return(composition)
}

# Extract species IDs and calculate lengths and composition
results <- data.frame(
  ProtID = character(),
  Length = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Store composition data separately
composition_list <- list()

for (i in 1:length(fasta_data)) {
  seq_name <- names(fasta_data)[i]
  # Extract ProtID (pattern: SpecN|PROTID/coordinates - remove /coordinates and anything after)
  # Example: "Spec1768|WWC61638.1/1-112 WWC61638.1..." -> "Spec1768|WWC61638.1"
  prot_id <- sub("/.*", "", seq_name)  # Remove everything from "/" onwards
  prot_id <- sub("\\s.*", "", prot_id)  # Remove everything from space onwards
  
  # Get sequence
  seq_string <- as.character(fasta_data[[i]])
  
  # Calculate length
  length_val <- calculate_nterm_length(seq_string)
  
  # Calculate composition
  composition <- calculate_aa_composition(seq_string)
  
  results <- rbind(results, data.frame(ProtID = prot_id, Length = length_val))
  composition_list[[prot_id]] <- composition
}

# Convert composition list to data frame
composition_df <- do.call(rbind, composition_list)
composition_df <- as.data.frame(composition_df)
composition_df$ProtID <- rownames(composition_df)
rownames(composition_df) <- NULL

# Check a few examples
cat("Example ProtIDs extracted:\n")
print(head(results$ProtID, 10))

# Merge with taxonomy
data_merged <- merge(results, taxonomy, by = "ProtID", all.x = TRUE)

# Remove entries without taxonomy info
data_merged <- data_merged[!is.na(data_merged$Subphylum), ]

# Print data dimensions
cat("\n=== Data Summary ===\n")
cat("Total sequences processed:", nrow(results), "\n")
cat("Sequences with taxonomy info:", nrow(data_merged), "\n")
cat("Sequences without taxonomy info:", sum(is.na(merge(results, taxonomy, by = "ProtID", all.x = TRUE)$Subphylum)), "\n\n")

# Print summary statistics
cat("Length Summary Statistics:\n")
cat("Min:", min(data_merged$Length), "\n")
cat("Max:", max(data_merged$Length), "\n")
cat("Mean:", round(mean(data_merged$Length), 2), "\n")
cat("Median:", median(data_merged$Length), "\n\n")

cat("Counts by Subphylum:\n")
print(table(data_merged$Subphylum))

# Define color palette for clades
clade_colors <- c(
  "Ascomycota" = "#E41A1C",
  "Basidiomycota" = "#377EB8",
  "Mucoromycota" = "#4DAF4A",
  "Zoopagomycota" = "#984EA3",
  "Chytridiomycota" = "#FF7F00",
  "Blastocladiomycota" = "#FFFF33",
  "Neocallimastigomycota" = "#A65628"
)

# Create histogram
p <- ggplot(data_merged, aes(x = Length, fill = Subphylum)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "N-terminal Length Variation Across Fungal Clades",
       x = "Protein Length (amino acids)",
       y = "Count",
       fill = "Subphylum") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.position = "right",
        axis.line = element_line(linewidth = 0.8),
        axis.ticks = element_line(linewidth = 0.8)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = clade_colors, 
                    na.value = "gray50")

# Display plot
print(p)

# Save plot
ggsave("nterm_length_histogram.png", p, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
ggsave("nterm_length_histogram.pdf", 
       p, 
       width = 8, 
       height = 6, 
       device = cairo_pdf)

# Optional: Create a faceted version by subphylum
p2 <- ggplot(data_merged, aes(x = Length, fill = Subphylum)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~Subphylum, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  labs(title = "N-terminal Length Variation by Fungal Clade",
       x = "Protein Length (amino acids)",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10, color = "black"),
        legend.position = "none",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = "black"),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = clade_colors, na.value = "gray50")

print(p2)
ggsave("nterm_length_histogram_faceted.png", p2, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# ===== AMINO ACID COMPOSITION ANALYSIS =====

# Merge composition with taxonomy
composition_merged <- merge(composition_df, taxonomy, by = "ProtID", all.x = TRUE)
composition_merged <- composition_merged[!is.na(composition_merged$Subphylum), ]

# Calculate key amino acid groups
composition_merged$Proline <- composition_merged$P
composition_merged$Serine <- composition_merged$S
composition_merged$Acidic <- composition_merged$D + composition_merged$E
composition_merged$Basic <- composition_merged$K + composition_merged$R
composition_merged$Hydrophobic <- composition_merged$A + composition_merged$V + 
                                   composition_merged$I + composition_merged$L + 
                                   composition_merged$M + composition_merged$F + 
                                   composition_merged$W

# Calculate mean composition by subphylum
comp_by_clade <- composition_merged %>%
  group_by(Subphylum) %>%
  summarise(
    Proline = mean(Proline, na.rm = TRUE),
    Serine = mean(Serine, na.rm = TRUE),
    Acidic = mean(Acidic, na.rm = TRUE),
    Basic = mean(Basic, na.rm = TRUE),
    Hydrophobic = mean(Hydrophobic, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n()
  )

print(comp_by_clade)

# 1. Stacked bar plot of key amino acid groups
comp_long <- composition_merged %>%
  select(ProtID, Subphylum, Proline, Serine, Acidic, Basic, Hydrophobic) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = c(Proline, Serine, Acidic, Basic, Hydrophobic),
                      names_to = "AA_Group", values_to = "Percentage")

comp_summary <- comp_long %>%
  group_by(Subphylum, AA_Group) %>%
  summarise(Mean_Percentage = mean(Percentage, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

p3 <- ggplot(comp_summary, aes(x = Subphylum, y = Mean_Percentage, fill = AA_Group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  labs(title = "Amino Acid Composition by Fungal Clade",
       x = "",
       y = "Percentage (%)",
       fill = "AA Group") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.line = element_line(linewidth = 0.8)) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Proline" = "#ECEFF1", 
                             "Serine" = "#CFD8DC",
                             "Acidic" = "#B0BEC5", 
                             "Basic" = "#90A4AE",
                             "Hydrophobic" = "#607D8B"))
  #scale_fill_manual(values = c("Proline" = "#F0F0F0", 
  #                           "Serine" = "#CCCCCC",
  #                           "Acidic" = "#999999", 
  #                           "Basic" = "#666666",
  #                           "Hydrophobic" = "#404040"))

  #scale_fill_manual(values = c("Proline" = "#E41A1C", "Serine" = "#377EB8",
  #                             "Acidic" = "#4DAF4A", "Basic" = "#984EA3",
  #                            "Hydrophobic" = "#FF7F00"))

print(p3)
ggsave("aa_composition_stacked.png", p3, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300)

ggsave("aa_composition_stacked.pdf", 
       p3, 
       width = 8, 
       height = 6, 
       device = cairo_pdf)

# 2. Boxplots for enrichment of specific amino acids
p4 <- ggplot(comp_long %>% filter(AA_Group %in% c("Proline", "Serine", "Acidic")),
             aes(x = Subphylum, y = Percentage, fill = Subphylum)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = 21, outlier.size = 1.5, alpha = 0.7, linewidth = 0.6) +
  facet_wrap(~AA_Group, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  labs(title = "Enrichment of Key Amino Acids Across Fungal Clades",
       x = "",
       y = "Percentage (%)") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.position = "none",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = "black"),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = clade_colors, na.value = "gray50")

print(p4)
ggsave("aa_enrichment_boxplots.png", p4, width = 14, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# 3. Heatmap of amino acid composition
comp_heatmap <- composition_merged %>%
  group_by(Subphylum) %>%
  summarise(across(A:Y, ~mean(., na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = A:Y, names_to = "AA", values_to = "Percentage")

p5 <- ggplot(comp_heatmap, aes(x = AA, y = Subphylum, fill = Percentage)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f", Percentage)), size = 3, color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Amino Acid Composition Heatmap by Clade",
       x = "Amino Acid",
       y = "",
       fill = "Percentage (%)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10, color = "black"),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
        panel.grid = element_blank()) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#2166AC", mid = "#F7F7F7", high = "#B2182B",
                       midpoint = median(comp_heatmap$Percentage))

print(p5)
ggsave("aa_composition_heatmap.png", p5, width = 14, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```
